Title: The m6A reader SlYTH2 negatively regulates tomato fruit aroma by impeding the translation process
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Lipoxygenase/hydroperoxide lyase branch producing C6 aldehydes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: volatile apocarbonyl / fatty acid-derived aroma volatile
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to tomato fruit aroma and consumer flavor preference
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in ripening fruit; transcript level not significantly changed in slyth2 versus WT
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: fatty acid hydroperoxides derived from polyunsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C6 aldehydes including E-2-hexenal and hexanal
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cleaves fatty acid hydroperoxides to generate C6 aldehydes involved in fruit aroma
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased SlHPL protein translation/accumulation in slyth2 mutants is associated with increased fatty acid-derived volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlHPL mRNA is m6A modified and directly bound by the m6A reader SlYTH2 at the 3' UTR. 2. SlHPL transcript abundance and mRNA decay were not significantly changed in slyth2 fruit, but its polysome association and protein accumulation increased in slyth2. 3. Increased SlHPL activity correlates with elevated E-2-hexenal and hexanal in slyth2 fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq; FA-CLIP; RNA-seq; proteomics; raw sequencing data under PRJCA024042
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-IP-qPCR; RIP-qPCR; EMSA; polysome profiling; proteomics; transient coexpression/protein blot in Nicotiana benthamiana; volatile metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 3' UTR m6A site mutation abolished SlYTH2-mediated translational repression in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: target pathway output can be increased indirectly by editing SlYTH2 to elevate aroma without obvious firmness or color penalties
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: m6A modification on SlHPL transcript enables recognition by SlYTH2 and translational repression via condensate sequestration

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Carotenoid cleavage branch generating apocarotenoid volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to tomato fruit aroma and consumer flavor preference
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in ripening fruit; transcript level not significantly changed in slyth2 versus WT
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: apocarotenoid volatiles including MHO, geranylacetone, and β-ionone
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidatively cleaves carotenoids to release aroma-active apocarotenoid volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: increased SlCCD1B protein translation/accumulation in slyth2 mutants is associated with increased carotenoid-derived volatiles
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlCCD1B mRNA is m6A modified and directly bound by SlYTH2 at the 3' UTR. 2. SlCCD1B transcript abundance and mRNA stability were unchanged in slyth2 fruit, but polysome loading and protein accumulation increased in slyth2. 3. Elevated SlCCD1B is associated with higher MHO, geranylacetone, and β-ionone in slyth2 fruits.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq; FA-CLIP; RNA-seq; proteomics; raw sequencing data under PRJCA024042
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: m6A-IP-qPCR; RIP-qPCR; EMSA; polysome profiling; proteomics; transient coexpression/protein blot in Nicotiana benthamiana; volatile metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: 3' UTR m6A site mutation abolished SlYTH2-mediated translational repression in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful downstream aroma enzyme whose output is increased by relieving posttranscriptional repression
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: m6A modification on SlCCD1B transcript enables recognition by SlYTH2 and translational repression via condensate sequestration

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fatty acid-derived volatiles; carotenoid-derived volatiles, including E-2-hexenal, hexanal, MHO, geranylacetone, and β-ionone
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: determines tomato aroma quality and consumer preference
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: fruit ripening stage (Breaker to B+7); LLPS assays with PEG8000 in vitro
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: YTH domain protein with intrinsically disordered regions and prion-like domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: highly expressed during fruit ripening; second-highest expressed YTH gene after SlYTH1 in Ailsa Craig and M82
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: translation repression via m6A-dependent RNA binding, liquid-liquid phase separation, and condensate-mediated sequestration
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: volatile biosynthesis through translational control of volatile synthesis enzymes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: binds m6A-modified 3' UTRs of SlHPL and SlCCD1B and represses their translation without changing mRNA abundance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: major negative regulator of tomato fruit aroma volatiles; knockout increases multiple volatile classes and consumer preference without obvious pigmentation or firmness defects
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: m6A marks on target mRNAs; fruit ripening developmental context
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: forms cytosolic condensates with translational regulators SleIF3C; SleIF4B; SlPABP
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SleIF3C; SleIF4B; SlPABP; SlHPL mRNA; SlCCD1B mRNA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: loss of SlYTH2 function increases total aroma-associated volatiles about 1.5-fold at B+7 and enhances consumer aroma preference
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlYTH2 is a bona fide tomato m6A reader with selective binding to m6A-modified RNAs. 2. CRISPR knockout of SlYTH2 increases aroma-associated volatiles from fatty acids, carotenoids, nonaromatic amino acids, and phenylpropanoids without obvious adverse effects on fruit color or firmness. 3. SlYTH2 directly targets SlHPL and SlCCD1B transcripts, repressing their translation rather than transcript abundance or stability. 4. SlYTH2 undergoes LLPS and enriches target mRNAs in condensates together with translation-related factors, thereby impeding ribosome occupancy and protein accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; in vitro RIP-LC-MS/MS; EMSA; in vivo FA-RIP-LC-MS/MS; m6A-seq; FA-CLIP; m6A-IP-qPCR; RIP-qPCR; RNA-seq; polysome profiling; proteomics; LLPS assay; FRAP; FA-IP/MS; BiFC; volatile profiling; consumer panel
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: m6A-seq; FA-CLIP; RNA-seq; proteomics; FA-IP/MS; sequencing data under PRJCA024042
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ constitutive promoter for SlYTH2-FLAG overexpression in transgenic assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SlYTH2 undergoes liquid-liquid phase separation and preferentially recruits m6A-modified target RNAs into condensates; unlike human YTHDF1 it represses rather than promotes translation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: no discernible differences in plant growth, flowering, fruit set, pigmentation, or firmness were observed in slyth2 mutants under the tested conditions
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: CRISPR editing of SlYTH2 is a promising strategy to improve tomato flavor by increasing aroma volatiles without obvious penalties in firmness or color
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: functions as reader of m6A epitranscriptomic marks on target mRNAs; translational repression is m6A-dependent
